summary: DHH announces the third and final beta of 'Agile Web Development with Rails' by Dave Thomas, noting it will head to publishers soon with a hardcopy expected around August. What started as a quick tutorial grew into a comprehensive reference covering the entire framework. He praises Dave Thomas and the contributing authors for their work, and highlights the fortunate timing with Rails approaching 1.0. He also thanks the approximately 2,500 beta book buyers and hopes their support encourages more publishers to adopt the beta book format.
key_points: The Rails book expanded far beyond its original scope of a 200-page tutorial into a comprehensive framework reference
The beta book format proved successful with ~2,500 early buyers filling a documentation gap when the community needed it most
The final release is strategically timed to coincide with Rails reaching version 1.0
DHH sees the beta book model as an innovation in publishing worth replicating
money_quotes: It's been quite a long process for something that was meant as a quick tutorial-type book at no more than two hundred pages.
Not only did the beta books hit just as people were crying out the loudest for more documentation, but the final copy will hit just as Rails reaches 1.0.
Once again, thanks a bunch to the ~2,500 people who bought into the idea of the beta book.
Hopefully this will encourage even more publishers to try out the format and reinforce the few others already doing it in that it's a great idea.
He has steered the book into an essential reference point for any Rails developer.
key_insight: The beta book format successfully bridged the Rails documentation gap at a critical moment, proving that early-access publishing can serve both community needs and commercial viability.
